mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. A sustained monthly increase in PAYEMS over 3 months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138037.0 thousand as of Feb 2013 reflects post-recession labor recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.02% (as of Feb 2013) represents a restrictive monetary stance relative to inflation expectations. A decline in FEDFUNDS below 4.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases forward equity returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle transitioning to easing
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses below 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth. The interaction effect produces stronger forward returns than either series alone over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1381.0 thousand and RSAFS at 377615.0 million as of Feb 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand recovery
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint signal produces positive 20d returns in < 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply-side momentum and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.03 (i.e., signal direction is reversed or near-zero), or negative INDPRO signals produce negative 5d returns in < 48% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annualized and FEDFUNDS remains below 5.5%, the central bank is in a real-rate accommodation regime that supports equity valuations. This interaction predicts positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 208.028 and FEDFUNDS at 5.02% as of Feb 2013
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10), or joint signal produces positive 60d returns in < 53% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.6% signals a tight labor market. A further decline in UNRATE below 4.5% indicates accelerating labor tightness that raises wage pressures and corporate cost burdens, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.02 (signal direction reversed or near-zero), or UNRATE < 4.5% produces negative 20d returns in < 48% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. A quarterly growth rate below 2.0% annualized signals economic deceleration that reduces forward equity earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth assumptions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11523.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2012
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or sub-2% GDP growth produces negative 60d returns in < 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both series accelerate simultaneously (month-over-month growth > 0.2%), this signals robust productive capacity expansion that supports higher equity valuations over 20 trading days through improved earnings quality and capital efficiency.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both show month-over-month growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.4254 and PAYEMS at 138037.0 as of Feb 2013
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor expansion in recovery phase
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or joint signal produces positive 20d returns in < 54% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
